title: The Circus

evidence.note: Published 1928; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — The Circus (1928), full film

editorial: The Circus is a 1928 American silent comedy written, directed by, produced by and starring Charlie Chaplin for United Artists, with the Tramp accidentally becoming a circus star; Chaplin received a special Academy Award for the film at the first Oscars. Published in 1928, the film print's US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry. Chaplin's 1969 reissue added a musical score he then composed, a separate later work with its own rights.
